choice: Yes — support a configurable annual cost drag applied as a per-step haircut to gross returns, defaulting to a single blended annual rate per Plan (e.g. 0.00–0.50%), not per-fund. Keep it as one knob in v1.
rationale: Fees are a first-order driver of long-horizon outcomes — a steady ~1%/yr drag can shift probability-of-success by 10+ points over a 40-year horizon, so omitting it silently overstates success. ProjectionLab and BoldIn both model a fee/expense drag explicitly. This is distinct from the bracketed tax model (dc489f83) and the spending stream (fb2dfbc8): it reduces the return series itself. The spreadsheet's historical returns are gross of any advisory fee, so this is a deliberate fidelity delta — worth a single explicit rate rather than per-fund modeling, which is OUT for v1.
